description_long: This is a weathered, wooden barrel or bucket with a wide, circular opening at the top, filled with a dark, granular or liquid substance. The exterior is constructed from vertical wooden staves, bound together by two visible horizontal bands, likely made of metal or painted wood, providing structural reinforcement. The surface shows signs of aging, with faded blue-gray coloration and some darker stains, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure. There are integrated side grips or shallow handles carved into the wood for easier lifting or carrying. One side features a patch or marking, possibly a painted label or repair, adding to its utilitarian character. The overall form is slightly tapered, wider at the top and narrowing towards the base, typical of barrels or large buckets used for storage or water collection in urban or garden settings.
description: A weathered, blue-gray wooden barrel or bucket with metal banding, filled with a dark substance and featuring integrated side grips and faded markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide opening, dark contents, and a visible patch or marking on the side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curvature, banding, and a more shadowed, darker wood texture.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with a clear look at the integrated grip and faded wood grain, no visible markings.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the continuous banding and another integrated grip, with the wood appearing more uniformly faded.
attribute_labeling_or_markings: faded patch or marking on one side
